scope: Competitor, licensing, provenance, architecture, and supply-chain intake. No implementation acceptance.

policy.clone_policy: Clone only after canonical origin and reuse-compatible license are confirmed; pin a commit; keep the clone in research/vendor-cache; never package or execute it.
policy.common_clone_reason: Official GitHub metadata plus pinned license, README, and dependency files and canonical papers were sufficient; cloning added supply-chain and packaging risk without adding decision evidence.

disposition_definitions.adopt: Schedule the mechanism or external baseline for a future Memory Lab intake; no code import is authorized.
disposition_definitions.adapt: Reproduce a bounded pattern behind Core-authoritative interfaces after benchmark, legal, and security review.
disposition_definitions.observe: Track evidence and maintenance; do not schedule integration.
disposition_definitions.reject: Do not pursue as an ATC dependency under current product and supply-chain constraints.

candidates.name: Hindsight

canonical_publications.title: Hindsight: Structured Agent Memory that Retains, Recalls, and Reflects

maintenance.evidence: Pinned main commit and push occurred on the evidence date; the official repository and releases show continuing feature, security, migration, and integration work.

architecture_to_borrow: Separate world facts, experiences, observations, and opinions.
Retain, recall, and reflect operations with inspectable intermediate memory.
Parallel vector, keyword, graph, and temporal retrieval with fusion and reranking.

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.11+, PostgreSQL/pgvector-centered service, LLM and embedding providers, reranking, optional database/search extensions, control plane, and multiple language clients.

license_caveats: The root implementation is MIT; hosted Hindsight services, trademarks, container bases, database extensions, models, and integrations retain separate terms.
Nested integration LICENSE files were present; every selected component and transitive dependency still needs an SBOM-level review.
safety_risks: The ACL paper says extraction and opinion errors can propagate through the graph.
The ACL paper says built-in PII detection and automatic redaction are not currently provided.
Persistent opinions can amplify bias or resist correction; memory-bank authorization and purge must remain Core-controlled.
candidates.decision_rationale: Best current full-system baseline and strongest epistemic separation, but too stateful and heavy to become a second authority.

candidates.name: Mem0

canonical_publications.title: Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory

maintenance.evidence: Pinned main commit and push occurred on the evidence date; repository metadata showed ongoing activity and a large active issue surface.

architecture_to_borrow: Additive salient-fact extraction rather than model-directed mutation of source evidence.
Deduplication and scoped memory CRUD.
Hybrid semantic, keyword, temporal, and graph-oriented retrieval patterns.

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.10+; OpenAI and Qdrant are base dependencies, with optional Chroma, MongoDB, Redis, Elasticsearch, Milvus, many LLM providers, local embedding/reranking models, server, and UI surfaces.

license_caveats: Apache-2.0 covers the pinned repository; the hosted Mem0 Platform and its data terms are separate.
Vector/graph databases, model providers, model weights, plugins, and container images have independent licenses and terms.
The archived mem0-mcp repository is not the maintenance target and was excluded.
safety_risks: LLM extraction can turn untrusted conversation text into false durable memory.
Provider and vector-store choices can create personal-data egress, tenant-isolation, and deletion-completeness risks.
Graph and platform state could become a competing authority unless outputs remain proposals or shadow results.
candidates.decision_rationale: Use as the first production-oriented extraction/retrieval competitor; adapt additive extraction only behind Core policy.

candidates.name: Graphiti / Zep

canonical_publications.title: Zep: A Temporal Knowledge Graph Architecture for Agent Memory

maintenance.evidence: Pinned main commit and push occurred on the evidence date.

architecture_to_borrow: Episode provenance separated from entity and relationship projections.
Bitemporal facts that distinguish event-valid time from system-known time.
Incremental invalidation and hybrid semantic, full-text, and graph retrieval.
integration_surface: Neo4j, FalkorDB, Neptune/OpenSearch, and deprecated Kuzu backends

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.10+, Neo4j base dependency, OpenAI base dependency, optional graph backends and local embeddings, plus server/MCP and container deployment surfaces. The pinned project marks Kuzu deprecated because upstream is unmaintained.

license_caveats: Apache-2.0 covers Graphiti at the pinned revision, not the hosted Zep product or service terms.
Graph databases, cloud services, model providers, and embedding/reranking models require separate review.
safety_risks: Entity resolution and fact invalidation are LLM-influenced derived claims and can create false relationships.
Graph traversal can widen disclosure unless Core authorizes IDs before every retrieval channel.
Multi-tenant group identifiers are not a substitute for ATC permissions or purge verification.
candidates.decision_rationale: Best temporal-graph reference; adopt the baseline and data-model lessons, not its graph database as canonical truth.

candidates.name: LangMem / LangGraph

maintenance.evidence: Both pinned repositories had July 2026 main-branch commits; LangGraph has frequent releases while LangMem remains active without GitHub releases.

architecture_to_borrow: Thread-scoped checkpoints distinct from cross-thread long-term stores.
Namespace-based memory isolation.
Explicit hot-path memory tools and background extraction/consolidation.
integration_surface: SQLite, PostgreSQL, and Redis checkpoint packages

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.10+. LangGraph core is modest, but LangMem requires LangChain, LangGraph, OpenAI and Anthropic integrations; durable production stores add database packages and services.

license_caveats: The pinned LangMem and LangGraph repositories are MIT.
LangGraph Platform, LangSmith, hosted deployments, provider APIs, and separate JavaScript packages are not licensed by these repository licenses.
The archived langgraph-memory example repository was excluded as a maintenance target.
safety_risks: Agent-invoked hot-path writes can persist prompt injection or model inference without a Core observation gate.
Checkpoint state can silently preserve stale or corrected memory unless dependencies and invalidation are explicit.
Namespace discipline alone does not enforce ATC authorization or minimum disclosure.
candidates.decision_rationale: Adopt checkpoint/store contracts and a future host adapter; keep every durable write subordinate to Core.

candidates.name: Letta / MemGPT

official_repositories.role: legacy Letta server and MemGPT lineage

canonical_publications.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems

maintenance.evidence: The canonical Letta README labels that Python server legacy and directs users to Letta Agent/App Server; Letta Code had a same-day pinned commit.

architecture_to_borrow: Virtual-context hierarchy with explicit movement between active and archival memory.
Agent-visible memory blocks, interrupts, and progressive disclosure.
Portable/versionable agent state and background curation.
integration_surface: Legacy Python API server and SDKs

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Legacy server requires Python 3.11-3.13, many LLM/provider packages, optional PostgreSQL/Redis/SQLite, tool sandbox and Docker surfaces. Active Letta Code requires Node.js 22.19+.

license_caveats: Both pinned repositories report Apache-2.0; Letta Cloud, hosted models, channels, skills, and third-party tools retain separate terms.
The repository transition means reuse review must target the actual active component rather than assuming the legacy server represents the current product.
safety_risks: Allowing an agent to edit its own memory blocks can conflate working state with canonical user truth.
AgentFile may serialize prompts, memory, and tool source; imports must be treated as untrusted code/data.
Tool execution and sandbox surfaces materially expand the supply-chain and exfiltration boundary.
candidates.decision_rationale: Adapt the working-set and checkpoint ideas; do not adopt the agent-owned truth model or broad runtime.

candidates.name: ReasoningBank

canonical_publications.title: Scaling Agent Self-Evolving with Reasoning Memory
ReasoningBank: Enabling agents to learn from experience

maintenance.evidence: Pinned main commit is from May 2026 while repository pushes continued in July; head activity is lower than the leading product systems.

architecture_to_borrow: Distill reusable strategies from both successful and failed trajectories.
Retrieve compact strategy memory before related tasks.
Use memory-aware test-time scaling to focus exploration.

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.13, Playwright/BrowserGym/WebArena, Torch, several model-provider clients, cloud credentials, Docker benchmark environments, and vendored patched harnesses.

license_caveats: The repository root is Apache-2.0.

The pinned tree vendors patched mini-swe-agent and WebArena source under third_party without nested LICENSE or NOTICE files. Their exact upstream revisions and carried obligations were not declared in-tree and require legal review before reuse.
Benchmark sites, datasets, cloud models, and containers retain separate terms.
safety_risks: Self-judged success can reinforce false or unsafe tactics.
Reasoning memories risk retaining hidden chain-of-thought, secrets, or task-specific personal data unless distilled to observable procedures.
Browser and software-agent harnesses execute effects and untrusted content and must remain outside this intake.
candidates.decision_rationale: Adapt only the outcome-linked procedure pattern; reject reuse of the bundled evaluation harness until third-party provenance is complete.

candidates.name: MIRIX

canonical_publications.title: MIRIX: Multi-Agent Memory System for LLM-Based Agents

maintenance.evidence: Pinned main commit is from June 2026 and repository pushes continued in July; the official release history describes a shift from desktop assistant to a memory-system API.

architecture_to_borrow: Explicit Core, Episodic, Semantic, Procedural, Resource, and Knowledge Vault memory types.
Meta-agent coordination of typed writes and retrieval.
Multimodal and resource memory separated from ordinary semantic claims.

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.10+, multiple model providers, image processing, async SQL, Redis, dashboard, Docker, and multi-agent orchestration.

license_caveats: The pinned repository is Apache-2.0; MIRIX Cloud and provider terms are separate.
The README credits Letta as a foundation. The absence of an obvious root NOTICE file makes exact derivative attribution a legal-review item rather than a finding of noncompliance.
Model weights, visual inputs, datasets, and container dependencies retain separate licenses.
safety_risks: Continuous screen capture can collect credentials, private communications, and bystander data.
Multiple memory-writing agents multiply prompt-injection and confused-deputy paths.
Local storage claims do not by themselves prove secret filtering, tenant isolation, or purge of derived multimodal artifacts.
candidates.decision_rationale: Strong additional candidate for typed and multimodal memory; adapt taxonomy only until privacy and authority controls are independently demonstrated.

candidates.name: HippoRAG 2

canonical_publications.title: From RAG to Memory: Non-Parametric Continual Learning for Large Language Models

maintenance.evidence: Pinned main commit is from July 2026 and the repository is not archived.

architecture_to_borrow: Entity/passage graph for associative retrieval.
Personalized PageRank over linked knowledge.
Deeper passage integration that balances factual and multi-hop recall.

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Pinned requirements include OpenAI 1.91, Torch 2.5.1, Transformers 4.45.2 and a broad research stack; model downloads and optional GPU use are material.

license_caveats: The repository is MIT; model weights, datasets, API providers, and downloaded artifacts retain separate licenses and use terms.
safety_risks: OpenIE can hallucinate graph edges.
Graph propagation can retrieve indirectly related private records and amplify disclosure.
It does not supply canonicalization, permission, correction, or purge semantics.
candidates.decision_rationale: Observe as an authorization-filtered retrieval channel; benchmark only after lighter deterministic and hybrid fusion baselines.

candidates.name: A-MEM

official_repositories.role: official paper evaluation implementation, renamed from WujiangXu/AgenticMemory

canonical_publications.title: A-MEM: Agentic Memory for LLM Agents

maintenance.evidence: The reusable library's pinned main commit is from December 2025; the official evaluation repository's renamed target had a March 2026 commit.

architecture_to_borrow: Zettelkasten-style contextual notes.
Dynamic tags, keywords, and links.
Relation discovery and memory evolution triggered by new notes.

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.8+, ChromaDB, sentence-transformers, NumPy, scikit-learn, and model-provider clients. Local embeddings can download model artifacts.

license_caveats: Both official repository targets report MIT.
The paper distinguishes reusable system code from evaluation code; provenance must preserve that split and the upstream rename.
Embedding models and Chroma/model-provider dependencies retain separate licenses.
safety_risks: Agent-driven evolution can rewrite older derived context and obscure the original evidence.
Generated links and tags can propagate hallucinated associations.
Deletion and correction fan-out are not demonstrated as authority-preserving or purge-complete.
candidates.decision_rationale: Observe the relation-discovery baseline; do not let its in-place evolution semantics touch canonical evidence.

candidates.name: AgeMem

canonical_publications.title: Agentic Memory: Learning Unified Long-Term and Short-Term Memory Management for Large Language Model Agents
Agentic Memory: Learning Unified Long-Term and Short-Term Memory Management for Large Language Model Agents

maintenance.evidence: Fresh peer-reviewed ACL 2026 paper; no official implementation repository was identified from the paper, ACL entry, arXiv entry, or targeted official-repository search.

architecture_to_borrow: Expose ADD, UPDATE, DELETE, RETRIEVE, SUMMARY, and FILTER as explicit memory actions.
Jointly optimize short-term and long-term memory behavior.
Use step-wise reward attribution for memory actions.
integration_surface: No reusable official software surface verified.
Paper describes a three-stage progressive RL and step-wise GRPO training pipeline.

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Training requires multiple rollout trajectories, reinforcement-learning infrastructure, memory-operation rewards, model weights, and benchmark environments; inference requires a compatible trained policy.

license_caveats: ACL publication text is available under the Anthology's publication terms, but that is not a software license.
No official code repository, commit, or software reuse license was located; implementation reuse is not authorized by this intake.
safety_risks: A learned agent policy directly choosing durable add/update/delete actions conflicts with Core authority.
Reward models can optimize benchmark behavior while learning unsafe retention or deletion policies.
Private-gradient attribution and purge would be opaque without record-owned or rebuildable learned state.
candidates.decision_rationale: Genuinely stronger current research candidate, but observe only until official code, licensing, reproducibility, and authority-preserving adaptation exist.
candidates.repository_gap: No official implementation repository found; exact commit pin is not applicable.

candidates.name: MemOS

canonical_publications.title: MemOS: A Memory OS for AI System

maintenance.evidence: Pinned main commit and push occurred on the evidence date.

architecture_to_borrow: Versioned MemCube memory units with provenance metadata.
Explicit memory lifecycle, scheduling, migration, and composition vocabulary.
Separation of plaintext, activation, and parameter memory.
integration_surface: Neo4j, Redis, Milvus, Qdrant, and other optional stores

runtime_dependency_burden.details: Python 3.10+, OpenAI/Ollama/Transformers base dependencies and a broad optional matrix including graph/vector databases, Torch, sentence-transformers, parsers, services, plugins, and applications.

license_caveats: The pinned repository is Apache-2.0; nested cloud plugins/apps, hosted services, models, datasets, databases, and parameter-memory artifacts require component-level review.
An Apache software license does not establish rights to redistribute separately downloaded weights or data.
safety_risks: Activation and parameter memory can retain private influence without record-level inspection or deterministic purge.
Unified scheduling can create a second memory authority and broad invalidation fan-out.
The backend/plugin matrix expands network egress, telemetry, secret, and transitive dependency risk.
candidates.decision_rationale: Reject as a product dependency; its scope and learned-memory forms conflict with ATC's local, cross-platform, inspectable, purgeable boundary.

cross_cutting_unresolved_questions: ReasoningBank third_party trees need exact upstream revisions and license/notice reconciliation.
MIRIX's Letta-derived code needs component attribution and NOTICE review.
Every future model, reranker, database extension, image, dataset, container, and hosted API needs a separate license and data-processing review.
Trademark policies for candidate names were not legal-reviewed.
No candidate has yet demonstrated ATC's authorization-before-retrieval, correction closure, purge closure, secret refusal, or no-egress requirements under an ATC-controlled test.
AgeMem has no verified official implementation repository or software license.
